steps.title: Spot the verb + object + infinitive structure
steps.content: The sentence reads “Land [blank] his technology to invent...” This structure—where a verb takes a direct object followed by an infinitive—requires a main verb in this position.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
The main verb must be finite (fully conjugated). Choice A, “used,” is finite: “Land used his technology to invent the world's first instant camera.” Choices B and C are infinitive forms, and choice D is a participle; none of them can function as the main verb.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
